input.title: Telemedicine Platform
input.description: Telemedicine Platform is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Node.js, PostgreSQL, WebRTC for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Patient Onboarding
requirements: Register with personal and insurance details
Health questionnaire and medical history form

FR-02.title: Doctor Search and Selection
requirements: Search doctors by specialty, language, and availability
View doctor profile with credentials and reviews
Sort by rating and next available slot

FR-03.title: Video Consultation
requirements: HD video call between patient and doctor
Screen sharing for reports and images

FR-04.title: Appointment Scheduling
requirements: Urgent care queue for immediate consultations
Appointment reminders via SMS and email

FR-05.title: E-Prescription
requirements: Prescriptions sent to patient's preferred pharmacy

FR-07.title: Payment and Insurance
requirements: Payment receipt and claim status tracking

FR-08.title: Chat Messaging
requirements: Chat available before and after consultation

FR-09.title: Health Monitoring Integration
requirements: Connect wearable devices for vital signs
Display heart rate, BP, glucose trends

FR-01.title: Design Patient Onboarding UI
FR-01.description: Design the user interface for patient onboarding. Create the screens, forms, dashboards, or status views needed by the SRS and make the workflow clear, responsive, and accessible for the target users.
acceptance_criteria: UI design for patient onboarding is complete and reviewable.
The patient onboarding design is responsive and accessible.
The design for patient onboarding aligns with the documented user flow and validation states.

FR-01.title: Frontend Implementation of Patient Onboarding
FR-01.description: Implement the frontend behavior for patient onboarding. Build the required user interactions, validation states, loading states, and error handling so the interface supports these behaviors: Register with personal and insurance details; Health questionnaire and medical history form; ID verification for identity confirmation.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend implements the required patient onboarding user interactions.
Validation, loading, and error states for patient onboarding are handled in the UI.
Frontend behavior for patient onboarding is integrated correctly with backend APIs.

FR-01.title: Backend Implementation of Patient Onboarding
FR-01.description: Implement the backend logic for patient onboarding. Build the required APIs, business rules, validation, permissions, persistence, and processing needed to support these behaviors: Register with personal and insurance details; Health questionnaire and medical history form; ID verification for identity confirmation.
acceptance_criteria: Backend logic for patient onboarding is implemented and testable.
APIs and business rules for patient onboarding enforce validation and security requirements.
Data handling for patient onboarding is reliable and supports the required workflow.

FR-01.title: Database Design for Patient Onboarding
FR-01.description: Design the data model and persistence strategy for patient onboarding. Define entities, relationships, indexes, retention rules, and migration changes needed to store and query the workflow reliably.
acceptance_criteria: The data model for patient onboarding supports the required workflow.
Relationships, constraints, and indexes for patient onboarding are defined correctly.
Schema changes for patient onboarding can be applied safely in target environments.

FR-01.title: Security Hardening for Patient Onboarding
FR-01.description: Implement the security controls required for patient onboarding. Cover authentication, authorization, encryption, auditing, abuse prevention, and compliance checks where the SRS or workflow calls for them.
acceptance_criteria: Security controls for patient onboarding enforce the required authentication, authorization, or data-protection rules.
Sensitive operations and failure scenarios for patient onboarding are logged, validated, and protected against misuse.
The implementation of patient onboarding satisfies the relevant privacy, audit, or compliance expectations in the SRS.

FR-01.title: Integrate Patient Onboarding with External Services
FR-01.description: Integrate patient onboarding with the external services or providers referenced by the SRS. Implement secure service connections, payload validation, error handling, and recovery behavior for any third-party dependency involved in the workflow.
acceptance_criteria: External or cross-system integrations required by patient onboarding exchange the expected payloads successfully.
Retry, timeout, and failure handling for patient onboarding integrations are implemented and observable.
Credentials, tokens, and outbound calls used by patient onboarding are configured and secured correctly.

FR-01.title: Testing Patient Onboarding Functionality
FR-01.description: Create and execute tests for patient onboarding. Cover the main success flows, failure scenarios, permission checks, validation rules, integrations, and operational edge cases described in the SRS so the implementation is safe to release.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover the main success and failure scenarios for patient onboarding.
Security, validation, and permission checks for patient onboarding are verified.
Regression coverage exists for the most important patient onboarding behaviors.
